title: Past-only latent degradation state instead of more threshold rules
summary: Recent F1 research models tyre pace as a latent state with structural resets at pit stops, which is closer to the true mechanism than static expected-life cutoffs.
rationale: Recent F1 research models tyre pace as a latent state with structural resets at pit stops, which is closer to the true mechanism than static expected-life cutoffs. That suggests using filtered degradation or pace-loss state as a feature, updated only from prior laps in the same driver-race sequence.
implementation_hint: Within each driver-race stint, estimate a recursive latent state from `LapTime (s)`, `LapTime_Delta`, `TyreLife`, and `Cumulative_Degradation` after race-level normalization; reset at `PitStop=1`. Feed only filtered current-state estimates and one-step-ahead deterioration estimates into the tabular model.
expected_effect: Could separate ‘old but stable’ stints from ‘old and cliffing’ stints, improving timing around true stop windows.
risk: Easy to leak if the state smoother uses future laps; a full smoother is wrong here, so only filtering/online updates are acceptable.
